ITS SOD Experiments

2024-05-21

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "~/Documents/Postdoc/SOD_Microbiome")
library(phyloseq); packageVersion("phyloseq")

theme_set(theme_cowplot())

Load data

load("Data/ITS/filtered_data/sodexp.RData")
load("Data/ITS/filtered_data/sod_5asv.RData")

sod.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, 
                                              levels = c("untreated","SODtrt","wildfire"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated","Treated","Wildfire"))

sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod <- factor(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod, 
                                              levels = c("untreated","SODtrt","wildfire"), 
                                                  labels=c("Untreated","Treated","Wildfire"))


filtered_read_counts<-read.csv("Data/ITS/filtered_data/ITS_subset_track_reads_sod.csv")
summary(filtered_read_counts)
##       X                 input           filtered        denoisedF     
##  Length:98          Min.   : 17885   Min.   :  7061   Min.   :  6800  
##  Class :character   1st Qu.: 40243   1st Qu.: 30915   1st Qu.: 29725  
##  Mode  :character   Median : 53484   Median : 41394   Median : 40324  
##                     Mean   : 54526   Mean   : 42627   Mean   : 41837  
##                     3rd Qu.: 65020   3rd Qu.: 51747   3rd Qu.: 51113  
##                     Max.   :141318   Max.   :103696   Max.   :102097  
##    denoisedR          merged         nonchim     
##  Min.   :  6495   Min.   : 6361   Min.   : 6361  
##  1st Qu.: 29636   1st Qu.:28507   1st Qu.:28489  
##  Median : 40140   Median :39476   Median :39456  
##  Mean   : 41742   Mean   :40879   Mean   :40846  
##  3rd Qu.: 51027   3rd Qu.:50434   3rd Qu.:50376  
##  Max.   :102195   Max.   :99171   Max.   :99021
new_names <- sapply(strsplit(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_nameITS, "-"), function(x) {
  substr(x[1], 3, nchar(x[1]))
})

sod_5asv.ps@sam_data$Sample_nameITS <- new_names

Bar plot agglomerated by order-facetted by fire factor

dataITS <- sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 
  filter(Abundance > 0.05) %>%
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

abund_plot <- ggplot(dataITS, aes(x = Sample_nameITS, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
  scale_fill_viridis_d() +
  scale_color_viridis_d() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance",
    fill = "Phylum"
  ) +
  facet_grid(~factor(Fire_Mod), scales = "free_x") +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 20),  # Increase y-axis label size
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),  # Remove the x-axis title if needed
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20), 
    panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 20),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 20, face = "bold"),
    legend.key.size = unit(1, "lines"),
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(
    fill = guide_legend(
      reverse = FALSE,
      keywidth = 1,
      keyheight = 1,
      title.position = "top",
      title.hjust = 0.5,
      label.theme = element_text(size = 20)
    )
  )
## Warning in geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", color = "black", :
## Ignoring unknown parameters: `size`
abund_plot <- abund_plot + 
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) gsub("Sample_nameITS", "", x))  # Remove unwanted text

abund_plot

ggsave("Figures/ITS/relabundance_plot_relabund_phylum_wildfire_samples.pdf", abund_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Let’s summarize with boxplot

dataITS <- sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom(taxrank = "Phylum") %>% 
  transform_sample_counts(function(x) {x / sum(x)}) %>% 
  psmelt() %>% 

  filter(Abundance > 0.05) %>%
  #mutate(across(Kingdom:Species, ~ ifelse(is.na(.), "Unclassified", .))) %>%  
  arrange(desc(Abundance))

combined_plot <- ggplot(dataITS, aes(x = Fire_Mod, y = Abundance, fill = Phylum)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.5) +  # Boxplot with no outliers and some transparency
  geom_jitter(color = "black", width = 0.2, size = 2) +  # Jittered black dots
  scale_fill_viridis_d(option="H") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Relative Abundance"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 20, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 24),
    axis.title.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 24),
    legend.position = "none",  # Remove the legend
    strip.text = element_text(size = 24, face= "bold"),
    strip.background = element_blank()
  ) +
  facet_wrap(~ Phylum, scales = "free_y") +
  stat_compare_means(method = "anova", label.y = 40)

ggsave("Figures/ITS/relabundance_plot_combined_facet_boxplot_phylum_wildfire_samples.pdf", combined_plot, width = 30, height = 16)

Let’s look top taxa at order level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Order")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/ITS/top40_order_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameITS", fill="Order") + facet_wrap(~Fire_Mod, scales="free_x")
## Warning: `aes_string()` was deprecated in ggplot2 3.0.0.
## ℹ Please use tidy evaluation idioms with `aes()`.
## ℹ See also `vignette("ggplot2-in-packages")` for more information.
## ℹ The deprecated feature was likely used in the phyloseq package.
##   Please report the issue at <https://github.com/joey711/phyloseq/issues>.
## This warning is displayed once per session.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at family level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Family")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/ITS/top40_family_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameITS", fill="Family") + facet_wrap(~Fire_Mod, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s look top taxa at genus level

top40 <- names(sort(taxa_sums(sod_5asv.ps), decreasing=TRUE))[1:40]
ps.top40 <- transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(OTU) OTU/sum(OTU))
#ps.top40@tax_table[is.na(ps.top40@tax_table)] <- "Unclassified"
ps.top40 <- tax_glom(ps.top40, taxrank = "Genus")
ps.top40 <- prune_taxa(top40, ps.top40)

pdf("Figures/ITS/top40_genus_sod_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 34, height = 20)
plot_bar(ps.top40, x="Sample_nameITS", fill="Genus") + facet_wrap(~Fire_Mod, scales="free_x")
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s make a table of top taxa.

data <-
  sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Phylum") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed <- data %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom'. You can override
## using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed, file="Results/ITS/mostabundanttaxa_abundance_phylum_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data1 <-
  sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Genus") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed1 <- data1 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class',
## 'Order', 'Family'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed1, file="Results/ITS/mostabundanttaxa_genus_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

data2 <-
  sod_5asv.ps %>%
  tax_glom("Species") %>%
  psmelt() %>%
  as_tibble()

data_summed2 <- data2 %>%
  filter(Abundance > 0) %>%
  group_by(Fire_Mod, Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance),
            Prevalence = n_distinct(Sample)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(Total_Abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  mutate(Percentage = 100*(Abundance / Total_Abundance))
## `summarise()` has grouped output by 'Fire_Mod', 'Kingdom', 'Phylum', 'Class',
## 'Order', 'Family', 'Genus'. You can override using the `.groups` argument.
write.csv(data_summed2, file="Results/ITS/mostabundanttaxa_species_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Let’s look at spacial placement of different plots

pdf("Figures/ITS/location_of_plots_wildfire_samples.pdf", width = 20, height = 12)
map_plot <- ggplot(sod_5asv.ps@sam_data, aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude)) +
  geom_text(aes(color = Fire_Mod, label = Sample_nameITS), size = 2.5, 
            check_overlap = FALSE) +
  labs(x = "Longitude", y = "Latitude", title = "Sampling Locations") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 20), 
    axis.title = element_text(size = 16),
    axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 12)
  )
print(map_plot)
dev.off()
## quartz_off_screen 
##                 2

Let’s normalize our data in preparation for beta diversity analyses

sdt = data.table::data.table(as(sample_data(sod_5asv.ps), "data.frame"),
                 TotalReads = sample_sums(sod_5asv.ps), keep.rownames = TRUE)
min_reads<-min(sdt$TotalReads)

ied.ps <- rarefy_even_depth(sod_5asv.ps, sample.size = min_reads, rngseed = 100)
## `set.seed(100)` was used to initialize repeatable random subsampling.
## Please record this for your records so others can reproduce.
## Try `set.seed(100); .Random.seed` for the full vector
## ...
## 561OTUs were removed because they are no longer 
## present in any sample after random subsampling
## ...
strat_rel.ps<-transform_sample_counts(sod_5asv.ps, function(x) x / sum(x))

strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod<-as.factor(strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Fire_Mod)
strat_rel.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR<-as.factor(strat_rel.ps@sam_data$YrSinceTrt_BR)
strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP<-as.factor(strat_rel.ps@sam_data$Treatuntreat_EP)

Alpha diversity-use non-normalized or pruned data

Let’s start with t-test as point of comparison with anova analysis

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")))

factors <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Lineage_EP", "SoilBin", "VegBin")
ttest_list <- list()

for (factor_name in factors) {
  ttest <- data.table(alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric())
  for (alphad in c("InvSimpson", "Shannon", "Observed")) {
    ttest_res <- t.test(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad], var.equal = TRUE)
    p_value <- ttest_res$p.value
    ttest <- rbind(ttest, data.table(alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value))
  }
  
  ttest_list[[factor_name]] <- ttest
}

for (factor_name in factors) {
  cat(paste("T-Test Results for Factor:", factor_name), "\n")
  print(knitr::kable(ttest_list[[factor_name]], format = "markdown"))
  cat("\n")
}
## T-Test Results for Factor: Treatuntreat_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.3612219|
## |Shannon     | 0.5476219|
## |Observed    | 0.2630071|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: Lineage_EP 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.9588522|
## |Shannon     | 0.6113740|
## |Observed    | 0.8745392|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: SoilBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.5046827|
## |Shannon     | 0.6474023|
## |Observed    | 0.3703509|
## 
## T-Test Results for Factor: VegBin 
## 
## 
## |alpha_index |    pvalue|
## |:-----------|---------:|
## |InvSimpson  | 0.5988286|
## |Shannon     | 0.6122687|
## |Observed    | 0.0218206|

Let’s now proceed with ANOVA analyses and then compile our alpha diversity stats

set.seed(1)
anova_list <- list()

AD <- estimate_richness(sod.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod.ps)[row.names(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

AD <- data.table(AD, keep.rownames = TRUE)

AD <- melt(AD, id.vars = c('rn', c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")))

factors2 <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

plotted_factors <- character()
 
final_anova_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())

for (factor_name in factors2) {
  # Initialize an empty data.table to store ANOVA results for each alpha diversity measure
  anova <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(), pvalue = numeric(), df_between = numeric(), df_within = numeric(), ss_between = numeric(), ss_within = numeric(), ms_between = numeric(), ms_within = numeric(), F_value = numeric())
  
  for (alphad in c("Observed","InvSimpson", "Shannon")) {
    anova_res <- aov(value ~ get(factor_name), data = AD[variable == alphad])
    anova_summary <- summary(anova_res)
    p_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$`Pr(>F)`[1]
    df_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[1]
    df_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Df"[2]
    ss_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[1]
    ss_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Sum Sq"[2]
    ms_between <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[1]
    ms_within <- anova_summary[[1]]$"Mean Sq"[2]
    F_value <- anova_summary[[1]]$"F value"[1]
    
    anova <- rbind(anova, data.table(factor_name = factor_name, alpha_index = alphad, pvalue = p_value, df_between = df_between, df_within = df_within, ss_between = ss_between, ss_within = ss_within, ms_between = ms_between, ms_within = ms_within, F_value = F_value))
  }
  
  final_anova_table <- rbind(final_anova_table, anova)
}

print(knitr::kable(final_anova_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name     |alpha_index |    pvalue| df_between| df_within|   ss_between|    ss_within|   ms_between|    ms_within|   F_value|
## |:---------------|:-----------|---------:|----------:|---------:|------------:|------------:|------------:|------------:|---------:|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    | 0.2630071|          1|        96| 1.210310e+04| 916546.21887| 1.210310e+04| 9547.3564465| 1.2676910|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |InvSimpson  | 0.3612219|          1|        96| 1.048303e+02|  11956.99047| 1.048303e+02|  124.5519841| 0.8416587|
## |Treatuntreat_EP |Shannon     | 0.5476219|          1|        96| 1.648284e-01|     43.45077| 1.648284e-01|    0.4526122| 0.3641714|
## |Fire_Mod        |Observed    | 0.3205896|          2|        95| 2.197632e+04| 906672.99304| 1.098816e+04| 9543.9262425| 1.1513251|
## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  | 0.6078982|          2|        95| 1.257347e+02|  11936.08601| 6.286736e+01|  125.6430107| 0.5003649|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     | 0.6298837|          2|        95| 4.223628e-01|     43.19324| 2.111814e-01|    0.4546656| 0.4644763|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Observed    | 0.7514745|          4|        93| 1.872423e+04| 909925.08929| 4.681057e+03| 9784.1407450| 0.4784331|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |InvSimpson  | 0.4004706|          4|        93| 5.076031e+02|  11554.21759| 1.269008e+02|  124.2388988| 1.0214255|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     | 0.6728333|          4|        93| 1.074084e+00|     42.54151| 2.685209e-01|    0.4574356| 0.5870135|
write.csv(final_anova_table, "Results/ITS/final_anova_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s make faceted boxplots for our alpha diversity analyses

set.seed(1)
AD <- estimate_richness(sod.ps, measures = c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon"))
rownames(AD) <- gsub("\\.", "-", rownames(AD))

AD <- cbind(AD, sample_data(sod_5asv.ps)[rownames(AD), c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")])

variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")

plots <- list()

for (variable in variables) {
  p <- plot_richness(sod.ps, x = variable, measures = c("Shannon", "InvSimpson", "Observed")) + 
    geom_boxplot()
  plots[[variable]] <- p
}

plots_arrange <- gridExtra::grid.arrange(
  plots[["Treatuntreat_EP"]], plots[["Fire_Mod"]], plots[["YrSinceTrt_BR"]],ncol=1)

plots_arrange_with_text <- cowplot::ggdraw() +
  cowplot::draw_plot(plots_arrange) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Treatuntreat_EP", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("Fire_Mod", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) +
  cowplot::draw_plot("YrSinceTrt_BR", x = 0.28, y = 0.98) 
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
## Warning in as_grob.default(plot): Cannot convert object of class character into
## a grob.
ggsave(plots_arrange_with_text, path = "Figures/ITS", filename = "alpha_diversity_each_factor_wildfire_samples.pdf", 
       width = 16, height = 24)

Let’s also perform Kruskal-Wallis and Dunn’s Post-hoc tests in case our data violates key ANOVA assumptions

set.seed(1)
variables <- c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")
measures <- c("Observed", "InvSimpson", "Shannon")

final_kw_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                              chi_squared = numeric(), df = numeric(), pvalue = numeric())

final_dunn_table <- data.table(factor_name = character(), alpha_index = character(),
                                Comparison = character(), Z = numeric(),
                                P.unadj = numeric(), P.adj = numeric())

for (variable in variables) {
  for (measure in measures) {
    data_subset <- AD[, c(variable, measure)]
    formula <- as.formula(paste(measure, "~", variable))

    cat("\n=====", measure, "by", variable, "=====\n")
    kw_res <- kruskal.test(formula, data = data_subset)
    print(kw_res)

    final_kw_table <- rbind(final_kw_table, data.table(
      factor_name = variable, alpha_index = measure,
      chi_squared = unname(kw_res$statistic), df = unname(kw_res$parameter),
      pvalue = kw_res$p.value
    ))

    n_groups <- nlevels(droplevels(data_subset[[variable]]))
    if (n_groups == 2) {
  
      cat("(2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)\n")
    } else {
      dunn_res <- FSA::dunnTest(formula, data = data_subset, method = "bh")
      print(dunn_res)

      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_res$res
      dunn_table_sub$factor_name <- variable
      dunn_table_sub$alpha_index <- measure
      dunn_table_sub <- dunn_table_sub[, c("factor_name", "alpha_index", "Comparison", "Z", "P.unadj", "P.adj")]

      final_dunn_table <- rbind(final_dunn_table, dunn_table_sub)
    }
  }
}
## 
## ===== Observed by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 2.1674, df = 1, p-value = 0.141
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.26714, df = 1, p-value = 0.6053
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Shannon by Treatuntreat_EP
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.075332, df = 1, p-value = 0.7837
## 
## (2 groups -- Dunn's post-hoc skipped as not applicable)
## 
## ===== Observed by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  Observed by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 2.6976, df = 2, p-value = 0.2595
## Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'FSA':
##   method       from
##   confint.boot car 
##   hist.boot    car
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 2.6976, df = 2, p-value = 0.26
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │  -1.090149
##          │     0.4135 
##          │
## Wildfire │   0.728175   1.520695
##          │     0.4665     0.3850 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison          Z   P.unadj     P.adj
## 1  Treated - Untreated -1.0901494 0.2756474 0.4134710
## 2   Treated - Wildfire  0.7281751 0.4665064 0.4665064
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire  1.5206952 0.1283363 0.3850090
## 
## ===== InvSimpson by Fire_Mod =====
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  InvSimpson by Fire_Mod
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.27951, df = 2, p-value = 0.8696
##   Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data: x and g
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 0.2795, df = 2, p-value = 0.87
## 
## 
##                      Dunn's Pairwise Comparison of x by g                     
##                              (Benjamini-Hochberg)                             
## Col Mean-│
## Row Mean │    Treated   Untreate
## ─────────┼──────────────────────
## Untreate │   0.520729
##          │     1.0000 
##          │
## Wildfire │   0.111226  -0.259031
##          │     0.9114     1.0000 
## 
## FDR = 0.05
## Reject Ho if p ≤ FDR with stopping rule, where p = Pr(|Z| ≥ |z|)
## Dunn (1964) Kruskal-Wallis multiple comparison
## 
##   p-values adjusted with the Benjamini-Hochberg method.
##             Comparison          Z   P.unadj     P.adj

## 2   Treated - Wildfire  0.1112264 0.9114368 0.9114368
## 3 Untreated - Wildfire -0.2590312 0.7956112 1.0000000

print(knitr::kable(final_kw_table, format = "markdown"))
##

## |Treatuntreat_EP |Observed    |   2.1674012|  1| 0.1409643|

## |Fire_Mod        |InvSimpson  |   0.2795081|  2| 0.8695721|
## |Fire_Mod        |Shannon     |   0.2502535|  2| 0.8823851|

## |YrSinceTrt_BR   |Shannon     |   3.7416124|  4| 0.4421026|
write.csv(final_kw_table, "Results/ITS/final_kruskalwallis_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

print(knitr::kable(final_dunn_table, format = "markdown"))
## 
## 
## |factor_name   |alpha_index |Comparison           |          Z|   P.unadj|     P.adj|
## |:-------------|:-----------|:--------------------|----------:|---------:|---------:|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Treated - Untreated  | -1.0901494| 0.2756474| 0.4134710|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Treated - Wildfire   |  0.7281751| 0.4665064| 0.4665064|
## |Fire_Mod      |Observed    |Untreated - Wildfire |  1.5206952| 0.1283363| 0.3850090|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Treated - Untreated  |  0.5207296| 0.6025552| 1.0000000|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Treated - Wildfire   |  0.1112264| 0.9114368| 0.9114368|
## |Fire_Mod      |InvSimpson  |Untreated - Wildfire | -0.2590312| 0.7956112| 1.0000000|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Treated - Untreated  |  0.4116374| 0.6806052| 1.0000000|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Treated - Wildfire   |  0.4182362| 0.6757744| 1.0000000|
## |Fire_Mod      |Shannon     |Untreated - Wildfire |  0.1315222| 0.8953622| 0.8953622|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 1to2        |  0.3381504| 0.7352498| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 3to4        | -0.0103148| 0.9917701| 0.9917701|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - 3to4          | -0.3701258| 0.7112887| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - 5to8        | -0.2609033| 0.7941671| 0.9927088|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - 5to8          | -0.6960575| 0.4863928| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |3to4 - 5to8          | -0.2593838| 0.7953392| 0.8837102|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |10to12 - untreated   | -0.5889060| 0.5559243| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |1to2 - untreated     | -1.6173417| 0.1058046| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |3to4 - untreated     | -0.6096863| 0.5420697| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Observed    |5to8 - untreated     | -0.2716789| 0.7858689| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 1to2        |  1.0836256| 0.2785308| 0.5570617|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 3to4        | -0.4841648| 0.6282689| 0.6980765|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - 3to4          | -1.7728364| 0.0762558| 0.3812791|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - 5to8        |  1.2996003| 0.1937380| 0.4843450|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - 5to8          |  0.5472445| 0.5842108| 0.7302635|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |3to4 - 5to8          |  1.8463724| 0.0648382| 0.6483816|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |10to12 - untreated   |  1.0483290| 0.2944871| 0.4908118|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |1to2 - untreated     | -0.1226833| 0.9023579| 0.9023579|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |3to4 - untreated     |  1.7631554| 0.0778743| 0.2595810|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |InvSimpson  |5to8 - untreated     | -0.6428834| 0.5202998| 0.7432854|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 1to2        |  1.0513083| 0.2931170| 0.7327925|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 3to4        | -0.3931564| 0.6942040| 0.8677550|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - 3to4          | -1.6203739| 0.1051520| 1.0000000|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - 5to8        |  1.0168675| 0.3092165| 0.6184329|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - 5to8          |  0.2135947| 0.8308631| 0.8308631|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |3to4 - 5to8          |  1.4595134| 0.1444239| 0.7221193|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |10to12 - untreated   |  0.8716381| 0.3834058| 0.6390097|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |1to2 - untreated     | -0.3697078| 0.7116002| 0.7906669|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |3to4 - untreated     |  1.4532983| 0.1461409| 0.4871365|
## |YrSinceTrt_BR |Shannon     |5to8 - untreated     | -0.4486180| 0.6537072| 0.9338675|
write.csv(final_dunn_table, "Results/ITS/final_dunns_posthoc_table_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Beta diversity-PERMANOVA analyses-ASV counts transformed (rel. abundance)

set.seed(1)
mapping <- as.data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

mapping2=mapping[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

ASV_tables_bray <- phyloseq::distance(strat_rel.ps, method = "bray")

df_metadata <- data.frame(sample_data(strat_rel.ps))

df_metadata<-df_metadata[, c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR")]

pvals <- sapply(colnames(mapping2), function(variable){
  form1<-as.formula(paste("ASV_tables_bray", variable, sep="~"))
  res <- adonis2(form1, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999)
  pval <- res$`Pr(>F)`[[1]] 
  return(pval)
})

pval_adj <- p.adjust(pvals, method = "fdr")

save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis <- pval_adj[pval_adj < 0.05]
save.pval.adonis0.05_braycurtis
## Treatuntreat_EP        Fire_Mod   YrSinceTrt_BR 
##         0.00015         0.00015         0.00020
print(pval_adj)
## Treatuntreat_EP        Fire_Mod   YrSinceTrt_BR 
##         0.00015         0.00015         0.00020

Compile PERMANOVA stats

set.seed(1)
res <- adonis2(ASV_tables_bray~Fire_Mod, data = df_metadata, permutations = 9999, by="terms")
res_data <- data.frame(res)
colnames(res_data)[colnames(res_data) == "Pr..F."] <- "pr_f"
res_data <- res_data[-c(nrow(res_data)-1, nrow(res_data)),]

res_data$FDR_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "fdr")
res_data$Bonferroni_adj_p <- p.adjust(res_data$pr_f, method = "bonferroni")
res_data$Factor <- rownames(res_data)

res_data <- res_data[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p")]
rownames(res_data) <- NULL

beta_disp_results <- lapply(c("Treatuntreat_EP", "Fire_Mod", "YrSinceTrt_BR"), function(variable) {
  res_betadisper <- betadisper(d = ASV_tables_bray, group = df_metadata[[variable]])
  perm_test_res <- permutest(res_betadisper)
  pval_betadisper <- perm_test_res$tab$`Pr(>F)`[[1]]
  
  data.frame(Factor = variable, P_Value_BetaDispersion = pval_betadisper)
})

beta_disp_results_df <- do.call(rbind, beta_disp_results)

final_results <- merge(res_data, beta_disp_results_df, by = "Factor", all.x = TRUE)

final_results <- final_results[, c("Factor", "Df", "SumOfSqs", "R2", "F", "pr_f", "FDR_adj_p", "Bonferroni_adj_p", "P_Value_BetaDispersion")]

print(final_results, row.names = FALSE)
##    Factor Df SumOfSqs         R2        F  pr_f FDR_adj_p Bonferroni_adj_p
##  Fire_Mod  2 1.678533 0.04371542 2.171406 1e-04     1e-04            1e-04
##  P_Value_BetaDispersion
##                   0.073
write.csv(final_results, file = "Results/ITS/PERMANOVA_with_betadisper_maineffects_wildfire_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)

Let’s now examine beta diversity through ordination plots

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS", 
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.2561642 
## Run 1 stress 0.2687318 
## Run 2 stress 0.2590987 
## Run 3 stress 0.2535892 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04214045  max resid 0.2807025 
## Run 4 stress 0.2650408 
## Run 5 stress 0.265866 
## Run 6 stress 0.2529501 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02364769  max resid 0.2192256 
## Run 7 stress 0.2691886 
## Run 8 stress 0.2548257 
## Run 9 stress 0.2552331 
## Run 10 stress 0.2533442 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05336189  max resid 0.2760793 
## Run 11 stress 0.2595123 
## Run 12 stress 0.2649691 
## Run 13 stress 0.2637474 
## Run 14 stress 0.2591766 
## Run 15 stress 0.2552439 
## Run 16 stress 0.2529275 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04303294  max resid 0.2182191 
## Run 17 stress 0.2661274 
## Run 18 stress 0.2536568 
## Run 19 stress 0.2602249 
## Run 20 stress 0.2594813 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##      5: no. of iterations >= maxit
##     15: stress ratio > sratmax
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         color= "Treatuntreat_EP") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  labs(color = "Factor") + 
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))

plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

write.csv(ps_NMDS[["points"]], file="Results/ITS/ITS_NMDS_coordinates_persample_wildfire_samples.csv",row.names = TRUE)

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot (rel abundance dataset), with Fire_Mod factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.2561642 
## Run 1 stress 0.2687318 
## Run 2 stress 0.2590987 
## Run 3 stress 0.2535892 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04214045  max resid 0.2807025 
## Run 4 stress 0.2650408 
## Run 5 stress 0.265866 
## Run 6 stress 0.2529501 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02364769  max resid 0.2192256 
## Run 7 stress 0.2691886 
## Run 8 stress 0.2548257 
## Run 9 stress 0.2552331 
## Run 10 stress 0.2533442 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05336189  max resid 0.2760793 
## Run 11 stress 0.2595123 
## Run 12 stress 0.2649691 
## Run 13 stress 0.2637474 
## Run 14 stress 0.2591766 
## Run 15 stress 0.2552439 
## Run 16 stress 0.2529275 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04303294  max resid 0.2182191 
## Run 17 stress 0.2661274 
## Run 18 stress 0.2536568 
## Run 19 stress 0.2602249 
## Run 20 stress 0.2594813 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##      5: no. of iterations >= maxit
##     15: stress ratio > sratmax
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "Fire_Mod") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses

Now, we’ll look at ordination plot rel abundance-with YrSinceTrt_BR factor

set.seed(1)
ps_NMDS = ordinate(strat_rel.ps, 
                   method="NMDS",
                   distance="bray")
## Run 0 stress 0.2561642 
## Run 1 stress 0.2687318 
## Run 2 stress 0.2590987 
## Run 3 stress 0.2535892 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04214045  max resid 0.2807025 
## Run 4 stress 0.2650408 
## Run 5 stress 0.265866 
## Run 6 stress 0.2529501 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.02364769  max resid 0.2192256 
## Run 7 stress 0.2691886 
## Run 8 stress 0.2548257 
## Run 9 stress 0.2552331 
## Run 10 stress 0.2533442 
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.05336189  max resid 0.2760793 
## Run 11 stress 0.2595123 
## Run 12 stress 0.2649691 
## Run 13 stress 0.2637474 
## Run 14 stress 0.2591766 
## Run 15 stress 0.2552439 
## Run 16 stress 0.2529275 
## ... New best solution
## ... Procrustes: rmse 0.04303294  max resid 0.2182191 
## Run 17 stress 0.2661274 
## Run 18 stress 0.2536568 
## Run 19 stress 0.2602249 
## Run 20 stress 0.2594813 
## *** Best solution was not repeated -- monoMDS stopping criteria:
##      5: no. of iterations >= maxit
##     15: stress ratio > sratmax
plot<- plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, 
                         ps_NMDS,
                         labs(color = "Factor"),
                         color= "YrSinceTrt_BR") +
  ggtitle("NMDS ordination plot - relative abundance") +
  theme(axis.text=element_text(size=14), axis.title=element_text(size=14), title =  element_text(size=13), legend.text = element_text(size = 18))
## Warning in plot_ordination(strat_rel.ps, ps_NMDS, labs(color = "Factor"), : type argument not supported. `type` set to 'samples'.
## See `plot_ordination('list')`
plot_ellipses<-plot +
   stat_ellipse(type = "norm", linetype = 2) +
  theme_bw()

plot_ellipses
